36			Short: "Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging",
37			Long: `Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.
38	
39	Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
40	token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.
41	
42	The embedded DDx agent harness is named 'agent' and is always available without
43	installing external binaries. Use --harness agent or --profile cheap to route to it.
44	
45	Profile routing (--profile default|cheap|fast|smart) selects the best available harness
46	and model automatically. Workflow tools should prefer --profile over --harness to
47	stay decoupled from harness installation details.
48	
49	Examples:
50	  ddx agent run --profile default --prompt task.md
51	  ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt task.md
52	  ddx agent run --profile smart --prompt task.md
53	  ddx agent run --profile smart --model gpt-5.4   # explicit override; avoid by default
54	  ddx agent run --harness agent --prompt task.md
55	  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
56	  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md

58	  ddx agent capabilities agent

The parent Fizeau-boundary bead includes DDx skill references, and those are duplicated across `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, and `skills/ddx`. CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- .agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md:8-16 describes DDx orchestration and Fizeau routing ownership.
- .agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md:46-61 defines `ddx run` and harness passthrough constraints.
- .agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md:138-140 says `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough constraints only.
- .agents/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md:3-46 is the dedicated routing guidance; `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md` and `skills/ddx/reference/agents.md` should match it semantically.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, and `skills/ddx` for stale DDx-owned routing, model aliasing, fuzzy matching, quorum-in-core, or `ddx agent` compatibility-alias wording.
- Keep legitimate examples of explicit passthrough such as `--model qwen3.6-27b`, but state that DDx passes the raw string unchanged and Fizeau owns interpretation.
- Keep the skill copies in sync where the same reference files are intentionally duplicated.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit website generated command docs in this bead; sibling child covers website/docs copies.
- Do not change production CLI behavior.
- Do not edit unrelated non-DDx skills.

Implementation agents need durable specs before removing `ddx agent`, renaming DDx internals, or consuming Fizeau contract changes. Today the specs still mix the old `agent` vocabulary with the desired DDx/Fizeau boundary, so agents can reasonably follow stale instructions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
FEAT-001 still describes an `Agent Passthrough` structural mount for `ddx agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:70-84. FEAT-006 still describes the upstream invocation boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` and `DDx Agent Service` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:1-23. FEAT-010 still says layer 1 consumes upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. TP-020 remains a historical DDx-side routing test plan even though Fizeau owns routing. docs/migrations/routing-config.md:1-13 removes some old routing keys but does not yet document the full `agent.*` removal / Fizeau-vs-execution config split.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the specs before implementation. Required docs: FEAT-001, FEAT-006 or its renamed replacement, FEAT-010, ADR-024, TP-020, docs/migrations/routing-config.md, docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md, and DDx skill references. The specs must state: DDx owns task execution (`run`, `try`, `work`); Fizeau owns routing, provider/model discovery, native transcript/session rendering, and fuzzy model constraint resolution; `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are explicit operator passthrough only; DDx passes raw strings unchanged; `ddx agent` is removed without deprecation; and `agent` is allowed only for actual external AI agent concepts.

NON-SCOPE
No production code removal in this bead. No Fizeau implementation work. No compatibility alias design. No generated CLI docs rebuild unless the docs generator is part of the spec-doc workflow.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-61668fd3 for native harness `agent` -> `fiz` contract identity

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
DDx currently imports github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau from cli/go.mod:7. The consumed Fizeau v0.10.9 public interface already has ServiceExecuteRequest.Model/Provider/Harness/ModelRef/MinPower/MaxPower fields, but its contract still names native behavior around `agent` and its current matching only partially normalizes model IDs. Upstream work is tracked in ../fizeau: fizeau-61668fd3 for native harness `agent` -> `fiz`, and fizeau-400b57fe for fuzzy model constraint resolution.

PROPOSED FIX

NON-SCOPE

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-61668fd3 must be closed in ../fizeau before this bead can complete.
- fizeau-400b57fe must be closed in ../fizeau before this bead can complete.

1	---
2	name: ddx
3	description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
4	---
5	
6	# DDx
7	
8	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
9	document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
10	items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
11	forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
12	model-ref, and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower`
13	bounds; Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery,
14	aliases, fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session
15	rendering), a persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library
16	registry (plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
17	synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
18	(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
19	the DDx surface correctly.
20	
21	## How this skill works
22	
23	The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
24	router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.
25	
26	**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
27	matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
28	not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
29	guidance, not this overview alone.**
30	
31	## Vocabulary
32	
33	Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
34	exact definitions.
35	
36	- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
37	  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
38	- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
39	  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
40	  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
41	  `ddx bead status`.
42	- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
43	  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
44	- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
45	  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
46	- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
47	  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
48	  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
49	- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
50	  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
51	  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
52	- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
53	  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
54	- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
55	  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
56	  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
57	- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
58	  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
59	  raw passthrough constraints.
60	- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
61	  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
62	  finalization.
63	- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
64	  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
65	- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
66	  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
67	- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
68	  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
69	  below).
70	- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
71	  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
72	  branch on harness names.
73	- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
74	  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
75	  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
76	- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
77	  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
78	  personas.
79	- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
80	  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
81	- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
82	  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
83	  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
84	- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
85	  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
86	  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
87	  `ddx install <name>`.
88	- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
89	  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
90	  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
91	- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
92	  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
93	  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
94	  orchestration; that remains harness business.
95	- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
96	  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
97	- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
98	  `ddx upgrade`.
99	- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
100	  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
101	  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
102	  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
103	- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
104	  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
105	  via `spec-id`.
106	- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
107	  at its governing artifact.
108	
109	## Intent router
110	
111	Before responding, read the matching file.
112	
113	| User says / asks about | Read this file |
114	|---|---|
115	| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
116	| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
117	| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
118	| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
119	| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
120	| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
121	| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |
122	
123	If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
124	run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
125	user which concept they mean rather than guessing.
126	
127	## Top-level policy reminders
128	
129	These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
130	reference file; do not violate them.
131	
132	- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
133	  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
134	  bead history and cannot be audited.
135	- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
136	  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
137	  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
138	  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
139	- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
140	  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
141	  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
142	  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
143	  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
144	  branches.
145	- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
146	  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
147	  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
148	  manual parallel work should too.
149	- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
150	  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough

1	# Task Dispatch — Power, Persona, Passthrough
2	
3	> **Debugging routing?** Routing lives entirely in Fizeau. See `CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service` (in the agent repo, `docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`) for the public surface; routing internals are inside that module.
4	
5	DDx invokes task execution through the upstream Fizeau service. DDx does not
6	choose harnesses, providers, endpoints, or models on the normal work path. It
7	describes the work, forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness,
8	provider, model, model-ref, and profile unchanged, requests an abstract power
9	level, and lets Fizeau route. Fizeau owns concrete routing,
10	provider/model discovery, alias resolution, fuzzy matching of raw model
11	strings, transcript/session rendering, and all route-side errors.
12	
13	## Power bounds
14	
15	Normal execution should use power bounds:
16	
17	```bash
18	ddx run --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
19	```
20	
21	Power is an integer scale exposed by the Fizeau contract. DDx passes
22	`MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` bounds and treats them as ordering
23	constraints, not as model identities. DDx does not query a model catalog,
24	discover providers, or pin concrete routes on the normal work path. Fizeau
25	reports what it actually used:
26	
27	```text
28	running with qwen 3.6-27b (power 10)
29	```
30	
31	DDx owns bead retry policy. It decides whether an attempt succeeded using DDx
32	evidence: commits, no-changes rationale, merge/preserve result, gates, review
33	verdicts, and cooldowns. If a retry is allowed, DDx may raise `MinPower`.
34	
35	Fizeau owns route selection within those requested bounds. DDx may raise
36	`MinPower` on retries, but it must not pin a concrete model/provider on the
37	normal work path or infer route selection from a catalog.
38	
39	## Passthrough constraints
40	
41	`--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--model-ref`, and `--profile` are
42	allowed only as passthrough constraints:
43	
44	```bash
45	ddx run --min-power 10 --model qwen36 --prompt task.md
46	```
47	
48	DDx sends these values to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate them,
49	score them, use them for queue policy, rewrite them on retry, or use them as a
50	fallback mechanism. Fizeau owns any fuzzy matching, alias resolution, provider
51	fallback, or typed error for those raw strings. DDx stops on hard-pin
52	exhaustion; it does not remove pins, widen pins, call route-selection helpers,
53	or retry in a loop.
54	
55	## Execution layering
56	
57	The intended command layering is:
58	
59	- `ddx run` — one Fizeau `Execute` call with requested `MinPower`/`MaxPower`.
60	  No route-selection helper.
61	- `ddx try <bead>` — one bead attempt layered over `run`.
62	- `ddx work` — queue drain and retry policy layered over `try`.
63	
64	Route diagnostics are status/debug-only. Do not use diagnostic route decisions
65	in `run`, `try`, or `work`, and never feed one back into `Execute`.
66	
67	## Personas
68	
69	Personas are reusable AI personality templates. DDx injects a
70	persona's body as a system-prompt addendum to `ddx run` —
71	same persona, same behavior across every harness, because the
72	harness sees a flat system prompt, not a persona file.
73	
74	### Default roster
75	
76	The default `ddx` plugin ships five role-focused personas:
77	
78	- `code-reviewer` — security-first review with structured verdicts
79	- `test-engineer` — stubs-over-mocks, real-e2e, baselined
80	  performance, testing pyramid as shape not ratios
81	- `implementer` — YAGNI / KISS / DOWITYTD, ships tests with code
82	- `architect` — opinionated on when to reach for each pattern
83	  (monolith-first, data-model-first)
84	- `specification-enforcer` — refuses drift from governing artifacts
85	
86	See `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md` for the quality bar and
87	authoring guidance. Projects can install additional personas via
88	plugins.
89	
90	### Using a persona
91	
92	Directly at invocation time:
93	
94	```bash
95	ddx run --persona code-reviewer --prompt review.md
96	```
97	
98	Or by binding a role in `.ddx/config.yaml`:
99	
100	```yaml

23	// Exit codes for ddx try terminal outcomes. These are specific to ddx try
24	// and intentionally do not reuse the generic ExitCode* constants because
25	// the bead-execution outcome vocabulary is distinct from generic CLI errors.
26	const (
27		tryExitSuccess         = 0 // success or already_satisfied
28		tryExitPreservedOrNone = 1 // preserved_for_review or no_changes
29		tryExitFailed          = 2 // execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
30	)
31	
32	// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try <bead-id>" command.
33	// It targets a single specific bead through the full production execution
34	// machinery (claim → execute → land → review), unlike "ddx work" which drains
35	// the queue by picker priority and cannot force a specific bead.
36	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
37		cmd := &cobra.Command{
38			Use:          "try <bead-id>",
39			Short:        "Execute a specific bead through the production machinery",
40			SilenceUsage: true,
41			Long: `try executes a single named bead through the full production
42	execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.
43	
44	Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
45	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
46	  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
47	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
48	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
49	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
50	
51	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
52	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
53	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
54	  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers
55	
56	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
57	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
58	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
59	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
60	
61	Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
62	--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
63	constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.
64	
65	Exit codes:
66	  0  success or already_satisfied
67	  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
68	  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
69	`,
70			Example: `  # Execute a specific bead
71	  ddx try ddx-abc12345
72	
73	  # Execute with an explicit harness and model
74	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini
75	
76	  # Execute without the post-merge review step (break-glass only)
77	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing`,
78			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
79			RunE: f.runTry,
80		}
81	
82		// Routing knobs — identical to "ddx work" passthrough surface.
83		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
84		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
85		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
86		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
87		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
89		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
90		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
91		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
92		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
93		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
94		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
95		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)")
98	
99		return cmd
100	}

100	}

102	// runTry is the RunE for "ddx try <bead-id>".
103	func (f *CommandFactory) runTry(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
104		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
105			return err
106		}
107	
108		beadID := args[0]
109	
110		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
111		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
112	
113		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
114		// Runs before claim/worktree setup so the operator sees the warning before
115		// any bead state changes.
116		preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
117		f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
118			emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
119		})
120	
121		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
122		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
123		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
124		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
125		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
126		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
127		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
128		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
129		noReviewAck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
130		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
131		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
132		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
133		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
134		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
135	
136		if noReview && !noReviewAck {
137			return fmt.Errorf("--no-review requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing (break-glass acknowledgement)")
138		}
139	
140		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
141	
142		// Pre-flight: look up the bead.
143		target, err := store.Get(beadID)
144		if err != nil {
145			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead not found: %s\n", beadID)
146			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
147		}
148	
149		// Pre-flight: verify the bead is in a claimable state.
150		// Claimable = open, or stale in_progress (the loop handles stale reclaim).
151		switch target.Status {
152		case bead.StatusOpen, bead.StatusInProgress:
153			// allowed — loop's Claim() handles stale-heartbeat reclaim
154		default:
155			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not claimable (status=%s)\n", target.Status)
156			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
157		}
158	
159		// Pre-flight: check for unmet dependencies.
160		if unmet := unmetDeps(store, target); len(unmet) > 0 {
161			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead has unmet dependencies: %s\n", strings.Join(unmet, ", "))
162			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
163		}
164	
165		// Wrap the real store so that ReadyExecution() returns only the target bead.
166		// All other operations pass through to the underlying store unchanged.
167		forcedStore := &singleBeadStore{Store: store, target: *target}
168	
169		// Set up progress logging (same as runAgentExecuteLoopImpl).
170		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
171		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
172		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
173		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
174		var loopSink io.Writer
175		if lf, openErr := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); openErr == nil {
176			loopSink = lf
177			defer lf.Close() //nolint:errcheck
178		}
179	
180		// Worker-probe (best-effort server mirror; non-blocking).
181		probeIdent := workerprobe.Identity{
182			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
183			Harness:      harness,
184			Model:        model,
185			ExecutorPID:  os.Getpid(),
186			ExecutorHost: hostnameOrEmpty(),
187			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
188		}
189		probe := workerprobe.New(probeIdent, workerprobe.Config{
190			AddrFunc: serverpkg.ReadServerAddr,
191		})
192		probeCtx, probeCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
193		probe.Start(probeCtx)
194		defer func() {
195			probeCancel()
196			probe.Stop()
197		}()
198		if loopSink != nil {
199			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(loopSink, probe)
200		} else {
201			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(io.Discard, probe)
202		}
203	
204		// Process-local LandCoordinator.
205		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
206		defer localCoord.Stop()
207	
208		// Post-merge reviewer (on by default, skipped via --no-review).
209		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
210		if !noReview {
211			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
212				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
213				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
214				BeadEvents:  bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
215				Harness:     reviewHarness,
216				Model:       reviewModel,
217			}
218		}
219	
220		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
221	
222		// Determine whether zero-config auto-route applies (same logic as runWork).
223		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" && modelRef == "" &&
224			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile")
225		autoInferTier := noRoutingFlags && !projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
226		resourceChecker := buildCLIResourceChecker(projectRoot, f.resourceCheckerOverride)
227	
228		// Build the executor: either the test override or the real single-tier executor.
229		var executor agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor
230		if f.tryExecutorOverride != nil {
231			executor = f.tryExecutorOverride
232		} else {
233			executor = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, execBeadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
234				var inferredTier escalation.ModelTier
235				if autoInferTier {
236					if b, getErr := store.Get(execBeadID); getErr == nil {
237						inferredTier = escalation.InferTier(b)
238					} else {
239						inferredTier = escalation.TierCheap
240					}
241				}
242	
243				loopOverrides := config.CLIOverrides{
244					Harness:           harness,
245					Model:             model,
246					Provider:          provider,
247					ModelRef:          modelRef,
248					Profile:           profile,
249					Effort:            effort,
250					MinPower:          minPower,
251					MaxPower:          maxPower,

| `TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding` | `try_test.go` | `BuildLandingGateContext` is called when executor produces commits |
| `TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead` | `try_test.go` | Output contains bead:, status:, result_rev:, preserve_ref: fields |
| `TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2` | `try_test.go` | Status → exit code mapping: success=0, preserved=1, failed=2 |
| `TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop` | `work_test.go` | `ddx work --json --once` output contains attempts/successes/failures/no_ready_work/results |

## Prerequisite Lifts (AC#13 / ddx-ddacd4ff)

The following functions were lifted from `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go` into `cli/cmd/try.go`:

| Function | Previous location | New location |
|----------|------------------|--------------|
| `agent.RecoverOrphans` | `agent_execute_bead.go:168` | `try.go` (called before executor spawn) |
| `agent.BuildLandingGateContext` | `agent_execute_bead.go:206` | `try.go` (called in landing path when result has commits) |
| `agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService` | `agent_execute_bead.go:161` | N/A — `ddx try` operates per-bead (not loop), validation is service-side; documented as not applicable for per-bead invocation |

## Deleted Source Files (AC#1)

All `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` source files deleted:

| File | Subcommands |
|------|------------|
| `agent_cmd.go` | `ddx agent` root + `run`, `condense`, `list`, `capabilities`, `doctor`, `log`, `benchmark`, `replay`, `providers`, `models`, `check`, `route-status`, `metrics` |
| `agent_execute_bead.go` | `ddx agent execute-bead` → `ddx try` |
| `agent_execute_loop_escalation.go` | Escalation logic (moved to work_impl.go) |
| `agent_execute_loop_review_repair.go` | Review/repair logic (moved to work_impl.go) |
| `agent_executions.go` | `ddx agent executions` |
| `agent_metrics.go` | `ddx agent metrics` |
| `agent_models.go` | `ddx agent models` |
| `agent_providers.go` | `ddx agent providers` |
| `agent_route_status.go` | `ddx agent route-status` |
| `agent_usage.go` | Renamed to `usage_stats.go` (no cobra route, utility functions) |
| `agent_workers.go` | `ddx agent workers` |
| `agent_check.go` | `ddx agent check` |
| `agent_doctor_workers.go` | `ddx agent doctor` worker health |

## Command Mapping Checklist (AC#3)

| Old command | New command | Status |
|-------------|------------|--------|
| `ddx agent run` | `ddx run` | Covered by `ddx run` (run.go) |
| `ddx agent execute-bead` | `ddx try` | Covered by `ddx try` (try.go) with engine lifts |
| `ddx agent execute-loop` | `ddx work` | Covered by `ddx work` (work.go + work_impl.go) |
| `ddx agent list` | Removed | No replacement needed; `ddx run --help` documents harnesses |
| `ddx agent doctor` | `ddx doctor` | Covered by existing `ddx doctor` command |
| `ddx agent log` | Removed | Logs available via `.ddx/executions/` directory |
| `ddx agent benchmark` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |

| `ddx agent capabilities` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |
| `ddx agent metrics` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |
| `ddx agent usage` | Retired cobra route; utility functions in `usage_stats.go` | |
| `ddx agent workers` | Removed | Worker management via server admin |
| `ddx agent check` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |
| `ddx agent route-status` | Removed | Routing status via Fizeau layer |
| `ddx agent providers` | Removed | Provider enumeration via Fizeau layer |
| `ddx agent models` | Removed | Model enumeration via Fizeau layer |
| `ddx agent executions` | Removed | Executions browsable via `.ddx/executions/` |

240	func TestDDxWork_ReadinessUnavailableOutput(t *testing.T) {
241		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
242	
243		stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
244		stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
245			if strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: intake") {
246				return nil, fmt.Errorf("readiness service unavailable: connection refused")
247			}
248			ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
249			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
250			close(ch)
251			return ch, nil
252		}

254		dir := setupWorkIntakeFixture(t)
255		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
256		out, _ := executeCommand(root, "work", "--once", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
257	
258		// AC1: actionable readiness-unavailable line must appear in operator output.
259		assert.Contains(t, out, "readiness check unavailable",
260			"ddx work must print actionable readiness-unavailable output; got: %s", out)
261		// AC6: doubled prefix must not appear in operator output.
262		assert.NotContains(t, out, "pre-claim intake: pre-claim intake:",
263			"pre-claim intake prefix must not be doubled in operator output")
264	}

267	// intake classifies a bead as too_large, ddx work must not dispatch the
268	// implementer and must move the bead to operator-attention status.
269	func TestDDxWork_ReadinessTooLargeSkipsClaim(t *testing.T) {

276				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"needs_split\",\"rationale\":\"too large\",\"readiness_checks\":[{\"reason\":\"too_large\",\"verdict\":\"fail\",\"evidence\":\"AC spans three subsystems\"}]}"}`)}
277				close(ch)

func TestZeroConfigWork_DispatchesWithEmptyProfile(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)

	dir := setupWorkIntakeFixture(t)
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	_, _ = executeCommand(root, "work", "--once", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")

	stub.mu.Lock()
	executionSeen := stub.executionSeen
	executionReq := stub.executionReq
	stub.mu.Unlock()

	require.True(t, executionSeen, "ddx work must reach the implementation dispatch")
	assert.Empty(t, executionReq.Policy)
	assert.Zero(t, executionReq.MinPower)
	assert.Zero(t, executionReq.MaxPower)
}

Here is a summary of what was done and the current status:

---

## Summary

### Files Created (from preserved ref `refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-d8474e0e/20260510T013243-b86bf611-6400274b71d6`)
- `cli/cmd/cli_helpers.go` - new CLI helper utilities
- `cli/cmd/work_impl.go` - ddx work implementation (803 lines)
- `cli/cmd/server_workers.go` - server worker support
- `cli/cmd/execute_bead_test_helpers_test.go` - test helpers for execute-bead tests
- `cli/cmd/run_test_helpers_test.go` - shared test helpers (stubs for FizeauService)
- `cli/cmd/usage_stats.go` - renamed from agent_usage.go, stripped of agent cmd surface
- `cli/cmd/testutils_test.go` - test utilities
- `cli/cmd/try_test.go` - tests for ddx try
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go` - tests for ddx work
- `.ddx/executions/20260510T062935-e4ef6b7f/agent-cmd-test-migration.md` - audit document

### Files Modified
- `cli/cmd/try.go` - updated with prerequisite lifts (RecoverOrphans, BuildLandingGateContext)
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go` - updated to remove agent command registration
- `cli/cmd/agent_run_passthrough_test.go` - updated to work with new structure
- `cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go` - updated (no diff from HEAD)
- `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md` - docs cleanup removing ddx agent references
- `.agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md`, `.claude/skills/ddx/SKILL.md` - synced from source
- `.agents/skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl`, `.claude/skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl` - synced from source

### Files Deleted
39 `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` and `cli/cmd/*_test.go` files removed; `.agents/skills/cli/` and `.claude/skills/cli/` stale snapshots removed; 26 `website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_agent*.md` files removed.

### Fixes Applied During Re-land
1. Import path `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau` → `github.com/easel/fizeau` in 4 files (previous attempt had wrong module path)
2. `fizeau.HarnessInfo.IsLocal` / `.IsSubscription` fields don't exist in v0.12.0 → replaced with computed expressions using `.CostClass` and `.Billing`
3. `fizeau.FizeauService` interface changed: removed non-existent `ResolveProfile`, `ProfileAliases`, `ListProfiles` stub methods; added missing `ListPolicies` stub
4. Test assertions `executionReq.Profile` → `executionReq.Policy` and `stub.listProfiles` → `stub.listPolicies` (correct fizeau field names)
5. Synced skill copies from canonical source to fix `TestDDxSkillCopiedTreesMatchSource`
6. Upgraded `github.com/easel/fizeau` from v0.12.0 to v0.12.1 (required by go toolchain)

### Build Status
**CLEAN** — `go build ./...` exits 0.

### Test Status
- `./cmd/...` — **PASS** (all tests pass)
- `./internal/agent/` — 1 failure: `TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow` — **PRE-EXISTING** failure confirmed on the checkpoint commit before any of this bead's changes (fizeau maps harness "agent" → "fiz" internally). Not introduced by this bead.
- All other packages — **PASS**

- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database

- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

import json

    \"Read cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go to understand the command factory pattern.\\\\n\\\\nAdd a new subcommand 'ddx agent benchmark'\",
    \"Read cli/cmd/run.go to understand the command factory pattern.\\\\n\\\\nAdd a new subcommand 'ddx benchmark'\"

)

print('done')
"

-# Execute-Bead Protocol
-
-`ddx agent execute-bead <id>` creates an isolated git worktree from the base revision,
-runs the configured harness, and either merges the result or preserves the branch on timeout.
-
-The merge path calls `git merge --no-ff` to record execution history as a non-fast-forward
-commit. The preserved branch is named `execute-bead-<run-id>` and stored in the tracker.

input.command:
sed -i 's/"ddx agent execute-loop"/"ddx work"/g' \
  cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go \
  cli/internal/server/workers.go && \
python3 -c "
files = [
    'cli/internal/server/land_coordinator.go',
    'cli/internal/server/worker_ingest.go',
]
for path in files:
    with open(path) as f:
        content = f.read()
    content = content.replace('\`ddx work\` / \`ddx agent execute-loop\`', '\`ddx work\`')
    content = content.replace('\`ddx agent doctor\`', '\`ddx doctor\`')
    with open(path, 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
print('done')
"

input.command:
python3 -c "
import re

replacements = [
    ('ddx agent execute-bead attempt', 'ddx try attempt'),
    ('\`ddx agent providers\`', '\`ddx providers\`'),
    ('\`ddx agent list\`', '\`ddx harness list\`'),
    ('\`ddx agent doctor\`', '\`ddx doctor\`'),
    ('mirroring the output of \`ddx providers\` and\n\`ddx harness list\` / \`ddx doctor\`.', 'mirroring the output of \`ddx providers\` and\n\`ddx harness list\` / \`ddx doctor\`.'),
    ('ddx agent workers stop', 'ddx server workers stop'),
    ('ddx agent route-status', 'ddx route-status'),
]

files = [
    'cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go',
    'cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql',
    'cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go',
]

for path in files:
    with open(path) as f:
        content = f.read()
    original = content
    content = content.replace('ddx agent execute-bead attempt', 'ddx try attempt')
    content = content.replace('\`ddx agent providers\`', '\`ddx providers\`')
    content = content.replace('\`ddx agent list\`', '\`ddx harness list\`')
    content = content.replace('\`ddx agent doctor\`', '\`ddx doctor\`')
    content = content.replace('ddx agent workers stop', 'ddx server workers stop')
    content = content.replace('\`ddx agent route-status\`', '\`ddx route-status\`')
    if content != original:
        with open(path, 'w') as f:
            f.write(content)
        print(f'Updated: {path}')
    else:
        print(f'No changes: {path}')
"

input.command:
find .agents/skills/docs/ .agents/skills/scripts/ .agents/skills/library/ .claude/skills/docs/ .claude/skills/scripts/ .claude/skills/library/ docs/ -name "*.md" -o -name "*.sh" -o -name "*.ts" 2>/dev/null | xargs grep -l 'ddx agent' 2>/dev/null | xargs python3 -c "
import sys
files = [f.strip() for f in open('/dev/stdin').readlines()]
for path in files:
    try:
        with open(path) as f:
            content = f.read()
        updated = content.replace('ddx agent execute-bead', 'ddx try')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent execute-loop', 'ddx work')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent run', 'ddx run')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent list', 'ddx harness list')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent doctor', 'ddx doctor')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent log', 'ddx log')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent benchmark', 'ddx benchmark')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent condense', 'ddx condense')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent replay', 'ddx replay')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent capabilities', 'ddx capabilities')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent metrics', 'ddx metrics')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent compare', 'ddx run --compare')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent check', 'ddx check')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent providers', 'ddx providers')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent models', 'ddx models')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent route-status', 'ddx route-status')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent usage', 'ddx usage')
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent workers', 'ddx server workers')
        # Generic catch-all
        updated = updated.replace('ddx agent ', 'ddx ')
        if updated != content:
            with open(path, 'w') as f:
                f.write(updated)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'Error in {path}: {e}', file=sys.stderr)
"
echo "done"

-// `ddx agent compare` A/B runs. Tests drive the implementation per TDD.

-		description: 'Cost-tiered routing policies for ddx agent',

input.command:
python3 -c "
import os
files = [
    'cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts',
    'cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/multi-model-review.spec.ts',
    'cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/plugins.spec.ts',
]
for path in files:
    with open(path) as f:
        content = f.read()
    updated = content.replace('\`ddx agent compare\`', '\`ddx run --compare\`')
    updated = updated.replace('\`ddx agent run', '\`ddx run')
    updated = updated.replace('for ddx agent', 'for ddx')
    if updated != content:
        with open(path, 'w') as f:
            f.write(updated)
        print(f'Updated: {path}')
    else:
        print(f'No change: {path}')
"

.agents/skills/.ddx/executions/20260411T195303-5b6c0922/manifest.json:14:    "description": "ddx agent execute-loop fails on eitri: agent harness routes to z-ai/glm-5.1 ignoring local vidar config

ddx agent execute-loop consistently fails on the helix machine (eitri) with provider-level errors.
The execute-loop correctly selects and claims beads, but the agent harness never runs the work.
CRITICAL: ddx agent run --harness agent works perfectly on the same machine, pointing to a
model-resolution bug specifically in execute-bead/execute-loop.

PROOF: direct agent run works, execute-loop fails:
  ddx agent run --text "Hello" --harness agent --timeout 60s → succeeds with qwen3.5-27b on vidar
  ddx agent execute-loop --once --json --harness agent → fails with z-ai/glm-5.1 via OpenRouter 404

ROOT CAUSE: Model resolution in execute-bead injects --model z-ai/glm-5.1, overriding the
native ~/.config/agent/config.yaml provider config (default_provider: vidar, model: qwen3.5-27b).
The z-ai/glm-5.1 model is not defined in models.yaml or the native config — it appears to come
from stale execute-bead routing metadata. Even explicit --model flags on execute-loop don't
propagate correctly to the spawned execute-bead subprocess.

PROVIDER STATUS on eitri:
- vidar (http://vidar:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): WORKS — confirmed with ddx agent run
- bragi (http://bragi:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): DOWN — connection refused
- grendel (http://grendel:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): DOWN — connection refused
- openrouter (https://openrouter.ai/api/v1): FAILS — 404 guardrail restriction on claude-haiku

MACHINE CONTEXT (eitri, Linux):
- Hostname: eitri, paths use /home/erik/ and linuxbrew
- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml defines providers with default_provider: vidar
- models.yaml defines code-high profile → agent.openai: gpt-5.4 (OpenRouter surface)
- No ~/.ddx.yml config file
- No project-level .agent/config.yaml
- Codex and claude harnesses nearly out of quota — reserved for review, not bulk execution
- execute-loop was working previously (existing ddx beads show successful runs)
- z-ai/glm-5.1 string appears nowhere in source — comes from runtime routing metadata

ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES: No AGENT_PROVIDER, AGENT_BASE_URL, AGENT_API_KEY, or AGENT_MODEL set.
All routing comes from native ~/.config/agent/config.yaml.

IMPACT: ddx agent execute-loop is the primary execution surface for HELIX beads. If the only
working provider (vidar → qwen3.5-27b) cannot be used from execute-loop, entire queues drain
as failures. The loop records each with retry_after (6 hours) and retries indefinitely.

SUGGESTED FIXES:
1. Fix execute-bead model resolution: respect native .agent/config.yaml provider config or
   properly propagate the --model flag from execute-loop → execute-bead
2. Add provider health checks: ddx agent doctor should test the actual provider with a
   minimal API call, not just check binary existence
3. Fail open on provider errors: when the default provider fails, fall back to the next
   available provider in config rather than hard-failing
4. Guardrail error clarity: surface OpenRouter 404 as "provider unavailable due to
   account restrictions" rather than generic API error
5. Execute-loop resilience: when all providers fail, mark bead truly blocked instead of
   infinite retry_after cycles

FILES INVOLVED:
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go (resolveNativeAgentProvider, resolveAgentConfig, resolveModel)
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go (execute-loop model flag propagation)
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (execute-bead model override logic)
- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml (machine config with vidar provider)",

.agents/skills/.ddx/executions/20260411T200949-32150c11/manifest.json:14:    "description": "<context-digest>
Review area: consumer-facing execute-bead / execute-loop guidance. Evidence covers the shipped CLI help surfaces, the open docs gaps tracked by ddx-a4195a8e and ddx-7a01ba6c, and the need to teach humans and agents one coherent model: execute-bead is the primitive, execute-loop is the normal queue-driven surface.
</context-digest>
Implement the first complete consumer-facing guidance surface for DDx agent execution.

## Required changes
1. Update CLI help text and any nearby command docs so `ddx agent execute-bead` is described as the single-bead primitive and `ddx agent execute-loop` is described as the queue/supervisor command built on top of it.
2. Add or update user-facing operator docs and CLI reference surfaces so users discover `execute-loop` first for normal queue-driven work, with concrete examples for:
   - `ddx agent execute-loop`
   - `ddx agent execute-loop --once`
   - `ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval <duration>`
   - `ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id>` for bounded/manual runs
3. Update the ddx-bead skill and any relevant internal skill/operator guidance so agents are told explicitly:
   - planning/document-only beads are valid execution targets
   - execute-loop is the default queue-driven entrypoint
   - execute-bead is the lower-level primitive for targeted/manual execution
4. Document the result/close semantics operators need in practice:
   - which outcomes/statuses close a bead
   - which outcomes preserve and leave the bead open
   - which JSON fields automation should read (`status`, `outcome`, `preserve_ref`, `result_rev`, `session_id`)
5. Make the operator guidance consistent with the current merge model:
   - merge is the default successful outcome
   - explicit required execution documents are the automatic merge gates
   - `--no-merge` is the manual preserve override

## Boundaries
- Do not invent a new bead shape or merge-gate field
- Use the existing execute-bead / execute-loop / execution-document contract
- Keep the user-facing guidance aligned with the actual shipped command behavior and documented result surface",

input.command:
PRESERVED_REF="refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-d8474e0e/20260510T013243-b86bf611-6400274b71d6"

# Sync docs/helix/ skill snapshots from canonical docs/helix/ (already updated in worktree)
for skill_dir in .agents/skills .claude/skills; do
  for f in \
    "docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-020-prompt-templates.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-002-epic-worker-surfaces.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-04.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/plan-persona-rewrite-audit.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-005-metric-runtime-history.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-006-agent-session-capture.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md" \
    "docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md" \
    "docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-04-04-exec-model.md" \
    "docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-04-05-agent-token.md" \
    "docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-04-05-post-sprint.md" \
    "docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-04-05-v0.2.0.md" \
    "docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-04-06-repo.md" \
    "docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-04-09-repo.md" \
    "docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-04-10-repo.md" \
    "docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-04-20.md" \
    "docs/helix/tests/PROMPT-ENGINEERING-RESULTS.md"; do
    skill_file="$skill_dir/$f"
    if [ -f "$skill_file" ]; then
      cp "$f" "$skill_file"
      echo "synced: $skill_file"
    fi
  done
done
input.description: Sync docs/helix skill snapshots from canonical files

# Sync library files from preserved ref (they were updated there but not in current worktree)
for f in \
  "library/personas/README.md" \

26:+    "title": "FULLY retire 'ddx agent' CLI surface — no deprecation period; replace with ddx run/try/work",
27:+    "description": "PROBLEM
All 21 ddx agent subcommands remain live in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85 — none are Hidden, none are Deprecated. The migration to ddx run/try/work was supposed to happen but only ddx work was added. Operators still use ddx agent surface, and skill manifests + docs still reference ddx agent.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85: 21 cobra command registrations for ddx agent subcommands (run, execute-bead, execute-loop, benchmark, condense, capabilities, replay, list, doctor, log, etc.) are all live. None set Hidden=true or Deprecated.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: execute-loop and execute-bead are aliases; after C13 (ddx-387a0178), ddx work and ddx try replace these.
- .agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, skills/: skill manifests reference 'ddx agent run' and 'ddx agent execute-bead'.
- docs/: README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website/content/docs/cli/_index.md reference ddx agent.
- Depends on C13 (ddx-387a0178) which adds ddx try CLI — cannot retire ddx agent until ddx try exists.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Delete cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and all cli/cmd/agent_*.go sibling files.
2. Verify: ddx run + ddx try + ddx work cover every prior ddx agent operation:
   - ddx agent run → ddx run
   - ddx agent execute-loop → ddx work
   - ddx agent execute-bead → ddx try
   - ddx agent list → ddx agent list removed (or migrated to ddx harness list)
   - ddx agent doctor → ddx doctor
   - ddx agent log → ddx log (or removed)
3. Update skill manifests to use new commands.
4. Update docs (README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website CLI docs).
5. Update lefthook hooks if any reference ddx agent.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding new flags or subcommands to ddx run/try/work (out of scope).
- Deprecation period (user directive: 'completely gone').",

3. ddx run, ddx try, ddx work cover all prior ddx agent operations (verified by checklist in commit body).
4. Skill manifests updated to new commands.
5. README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website CLI docs updated.
6. Lefthook hooks updated if they referenced ddx agent.
7. cd cli && go build ./... green.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
10. For each ddx agent subcommand removed, name the test in cli/cmd/{run,try,work}_test.go that asserts the new entry point preserves the prior behavior. At minimum: TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn, TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding, TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead, TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop, TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2 (matches ddx-c8f79963 / try.Attempt disposition contract).
11. Test surface classification — cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead*_test.go (~3,491 lines: agent_execute_bead_test.go, agent_execute_bead_e2e_test.go, agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go, execute_bead_e2e_routing_test.go) and cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop*_test.go: produce .ddx/executions/<run-id>/agent-cmd-test-migration.md classifying each test as (a) cobra-flag-parsing-only → delete, (b) engine-via-harness → migrate to call ExecuteBeadWithConfig or try.Attempt directly, (c) unique behavior → re-target to ddx try / ddx work cobra surface with same assertions. Each row of the audit names the test function and the disposition.
12. DEPENDENCY ddx-424fdac4 provides the governing-doc authorization for removing the ddx agent CLI surface (FEAT-001 mount removal, ADR-024 routing-boundary ratification per ddx-3ee4ddcf NON-SCOPE clause). Verify ddx-424fdac4 is closed before this bead's removal step.
13. DEPENDENCY ddx-ddacd4ff (C14) AC #7-9 lift agent.RecoverOrphans, agent.BuildLandingGateContext, and agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService from the cobra wrapper (cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:160-217) into the engine or try.Attempt before deletion. This bead's full retirement step inherits those lifts as prerequisites.",

# capture-omlx-fixture.sh: ddx agent run → ddx run
python3 -c "
import re
with open('scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()
content = content.replace('ddx agent run', 'ddx run')
content = content.replace('ddx agent invocation', 'ddx run invocation')
with open('scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
print('done')
"

# eval-skill.sh: ddx agent list → ddx harness list, ddx agent run → ddx run
python3 -c "
with open('scripts/eval-skill.sh', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()
content = content.replace('ddx agent list', 'ddx harness list')
content = content.replace('ddx agent run', 'ddx run')
with open('scripts/eval-skill.sh', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
print('done')
"

# test-doc-snippets.sh: remove or update the agent lines
python3 -c "
with open('scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()
content = content.replace('check \"ddx agent list\"         ddx agent list', 'check \"ddx harness list\"       ddx harness list')
content = content.replace('check \"ddx agent usage\"        ddx agent usage', 'check \"ddx usage\"              ddx usage')
content = content.replace('check \"ddx agent usage --help\" ddx agent usage --help', 'check \"ddx usage --help\"       ddx usage --help')
with open('scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
print('done')
"

# demos/06-full-journey.sh: ddx agent run → ddx run, ddx agent usage → ddx usage
python3 -c "
with open('scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh', 'r') as f:

content = content.replace('ddx agent run', 'ddx run')
content = content.replace('ddx agent usage', 'ddx usage')
with open('scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
print('done')
"

replacements = [
    ("ddx agent execute-loop", "ddx work"),
    ("ddx agent execute-bead", "ddx try"),
    ("ddx agent run", "ddx run"),
    ("ddx agent workers", "ddx server workers"),
    ("ddx agent doctor", "ddx doctor"),
    ("ddx agent log", "ddx log"),
    ("ddx agent check", "ddx check"),
    ("ddx agent metrics", "ddx metrics"),
    ("ddx agent list", "ddx harness list"),
    ("ddx agent providers", "ddx providers"),
    ("ddx agent usage", "ddx usage"),
    ("ddx agent route-status", "ddx route-status"),
    ("ddx agent executions", "ddx executions"),
    ("ddx agent models", "ddx models"),
    ("ddx agent compare", "ddx compare"),
    ("ddx agent capabilities", "ddx harness capabilities"),
    ("ddx agent grade", "ddx grade"),
    ("ddx agent stop", "ddx server workers stop"),
]

for path in [".agents/skills/.ddx/beads.jsonl", ".claude/skills/.ddx/beads.jsonl"]:
    with open(path, 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
    original = content
    for old, new in replacements:
        content = content.replace(old, new)
    if content != original:
        with open(path, 'w') as f:
            f.write(content)
        print(f"Updated: {path}")
    # Check remaining
    import re
    remaining = len(re.findall(r'ddx agent', content))
    print(f"  Remaining 'ddx agent' hits: {remaining}")
EOF

<!-- Managed by ddx init / ddx update. Edit outside these markers. -->

# DDx

This project uses [DDx](https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx) for
document-driven development. Use the `ddx` skill for beads, work,
review, agents, and status — every skills-compatible harness (Claude
Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) discovers it from
`.claude/skills/ddx/` and `.agents/skills/ddx/`.

## Files to commit

After modifying any of these paths, stage and commit them:

- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` — work item tracker
- `.ddx/config.yaml` — project configuration
- `.agents/skills/ddx/` — the ddx skill (shipped by ddx init)
- `.claude/skills/ddx/` — same skill, Claude Code location
- `docs/` — project documentation and artifacts

## Conventions

- Use `ddx bead` for work tracking (not custom issue files).
- Documents with `ddx:` frontmatter are tracked in the document graph.
- Run `ddx doctor` to check environment health.
- Run `ddx doc stale` to find documents needing review.

## Merge Policy

Branches containing `ddx try` or `ddx work` commits
carry a per-attempt execution audit trail:

- `chore: update tracker (execute-bead <TIMESTAMP>)` — attempt heartbeats
- `Merge bead <bead-id> attempt <TIMESTAMP>- into <branch>` — successful lands
- `feat|fix|...: ... [ddx-<id>]` — substantive bead work

Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` pointers into this history. Any
SHA rewrite breaks the trail. **Never squash, rebase, or filter** these
branches. Use only:

- `git merge --ff-only` when the target is a strict ancestor, or
- `git merge --no-ff` when divergence exists

Forbidden on execute-bead branches: `gh pr merge --squash`,
`gh pr merge --rebase`, `git rebase -i` with fixup/squash/drop,
`git filter-branch`, `git filter-repo`, and `git commit --amend` on
any commit already in the trail.
<!-- DDX-AGENTS:END -->

663	// generateAgentsMD injects (or refreshes) the DDx-owned block in AGENTS.md.
664	// If AGENTS.md does not exist, it is created containing only the block.
665	// If AGENTS.md exists with markers, the content between markers is replaced
666	// and everything outside is preserved. If AGENTS.md exists without markers,
667	// the block is appended to the end.
668	func generateAgentsMD(workingDir string) {
669		agentsPath := filepath.Join(workingDir, "AGENTS.md")
670		block := ddxAgentsBlock()
671	
672		existing, err := os.ReadFile(agentsPath)
673		if err != nil {
674			// File doesn't exist — create with just the block.
675			_ = os.WriteFile(agentsPath, []byte(block), 0644)
676			return
677		}
678	
679		content := string(existing)
680		startIdx := strings.Index(content, agentsMarkerStart)
681		endIdx := strings.Index(content, agentsMarkerEnd)
682	
683		if startIdx == -1 || endIdx == -1 || endIdx < startIdx {
684			// Markers not present (or malformed). Append block to end.
685			separator := ""
686			if !strings.HasSuffix(content, "\n") {
687				separator = "\n"
688			}
689			if !strings.HasSuffix(content, "\n\n") {
690				separator += "\n"
691			}
692			updated := content + separator + block
693			_ = os.WriteFile(agentsPath, []byte(updated), 0644)
694			return
695		}
696	
697		// Markers present — replace the block between them.
698		endOfEndMarker := endIdx + len(agentsMarkerEnd)
699		// Include trailing newline after the end marker if present.
700		if endOfEndMarker < len(content) && content[endOfEndMarker] == '\n' {
701			endOfEndMarker++
702		}
703		updated := content[:startIdx] + block + content[endOfEndMarker:]
704		_ = os.WriteFile(agentsPath, []byte(updated), 0644)
705	}
706	
707	// injectInitialMetaPrompt injects the configured meta-prompt into CLAUDE.md
708	func injectInitialMetaPrompt(cfg *config.Config, workingDir string) error {
709		// Get meta-prompt path from config (with default)
710		promptPath := cfg.GetMetaPrompt()
711		if promptPath == "" {
712			// Empty means disabled
713			return nil
714		}
715	
716		// Create injector
717		injector := metaprompt.NewMetaPromptInjectorWithPaths(
718			"CLAUDE.md",
719			cfg.Library.Path,
720			workingDir,
721		)
722	
723		// Inject prompt
724		if err := injector.InjectMetaPrompt(promptPath); err != nil {
725			return fmt.Errorf("failed to inject meta-prompt: %w", err)
726		}
727	
728		return nil
729	}

+// fizeauHarness maps DDx internal harness names to the Fizeau wire contract.
+// "agent" is DDx's internal name for the embedded native harness; Fizeau
+// v0.10.12+ uses "fiz" as the native harness identity on its wire API.
+func fizeauHarness(name string) string {
+	if name == "agent" {
+		return "fiz"
+	}
+	return name
+}

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat!: retire ddx agent CLI surface — replace with ddx run/try/work [ddx-d8474e0e]

Remove all 21 ddx agent subcommands. ddx run/try/work cover every
prior operation:
  - ddx agent run           → ddx run
  - ddx agent execute-bead  → ddx try
  - ddx agent execute-loop  → ddx work
  - ddx agent doctor        → ddx doctor
  - ddx agent log           → ddx log
